Event: Stitches up Jack’s side wound
Explanation: Helps Jack medically with no evident downside to herself.

Event: Phones Mars on Holy Days to manage being on the run
Explanation: Contacting her pursuer risks her safety while providing him useful info/closure.

Event: Gives anguished declaration of love and kiss to Jack as he stays to die
Explanation: Provides emotional support and closure for both with little downside.

Event: Applies double standards and selfish behavior early on
Explanation: Her selfish choices benefit her at others’ social/emotional expense.

Event: Gets caught and used by the Others to send search group back
Explanation: Her capture harms her and partially misdirects the group.

Event: Acts sweet and innocent to get out of scrapes
Explanation: Her manipulation protects her at the cost of others’ informed consent/justice.

Event: Deals finishing blow to the Man in Black
Explanation: Eliminating a major threat protects her and others.

Event: Commits murder and repeated crimes in flashbacks
Explanation: Her crimes serve her aims while harming or endangering others.

Event: Volunteers to donate blood to young Ben
Explanation: Sacrifices comfort/health risk to save a child with no clear self-gain.

Event: Tells Sawyer “I’m up here” when he ogles her
Explanation: Reasserts boundaries and slightly pushes Sawyer toward respectful behavior.

Event: Cannot admit her feelings, confessing only under stress
Explanation: Her emotional avoidance mostly harms her relationships with little benefit.

Event: Engages in childhood romance/promise with Tom
Explanation: Mutually affectionate bond until his unrelated death.

Event: Repeatedly gets kidnapped by the Others
Explanation: Her frequent captures endanger her with limited benefit to others.

Event: Attempts to steal Sawyer’s place on the raft and pose as Joanna
Explanation: Trying to secure escape and false identity benefits her while deceiving and endangering others.

Event: Sawyer exposes Kate’s raft plan and criminal past
Explanation: Protects the group’s interest in a fair raft spot and informed trust with minor social fallout.

Event: Shoots the Man in Black as he is about to kill Jack
Explanation: Saves Jack and herself by removing an immediate lethal threat.

Event: Sutures Jack’s wound despite fear and uncertainty
Explanation: Overcomes fear to provide life-saving aid, also gaining confidence.

Event: Stages a bank robbery for her own benefit
Explanation: Commits major crime for personal gain while threatening others.

Event: Shoots her partner and accomplices to stop killing during robbery
Explanation: Kills allies, ending threat but also destroying her protection and worsening her legal position.

Event: Is aghast at Jack and Sayid torturing Sawyer
Explanation: Opposes unnecessary torture, protecting Sawyer and some group morality.

Event: Refuses to let child Ben die after Sayid shoots him
Explanation: Acts on principle to save a child despite risk that he’ll become an enemy.

Event: Harbors intense self-loathing that drives selfish decisions
Explanation: Her self-hatred pushes her into choices that harm both herself and her relationships.

Event: Agrees to lie about the Island on return to civilization
Explanation: Protects herself and the Oceanic Six at the expense of truth and potential public benefit.

Event: Assures Jack she’ll always have his back
Explanation: Provides loyalty and emotional support, strengthening both.

Event: Tries to talk Jack out of reckless actions (returning, not saving Ben, detonating bomb)
Explanation: Attempts to reduce risk to Jack and others with limited cost to herself.

Event: Gives Jack a last kiss when he is mortally wounded
Explanation: Offers him comfort and closure at the cost of emotional pain to herself.

Event: Willing to go to prison to keep Aaron from her toxic mother
Explanation: Sacrifices her own freedom to protect Aaron’s wellbeing.

Event: Kicks Jack out when his behavior becomes erratic around Aaron
Explanation: Protects Aaron and herself from Jack’s instability, possibly prompting him to confront his issues.

Event: Lectures Locke about love while refusing to rescue the man who loved her and Aaron’s real mother
Explanation: Claims moral high ground while prioritizing her own emotional/security needs over fairness to others.

Event: Murders Wayne by blowing up the house
Explanation: Removes her abuser and personal tormentor through lethal violence and property destruction.

Event: Feels down when ostracized after her crimes are exposed
Explanation: Her hurt feelings stem from justified reactions to her own past, harming only herself.

Event: Adopts many aliases while on the run
Explanation: False identities help her evade capture while obstructing justice.

Event: Murdering Wayne out of disgust at his being her biological father
Explanation: Eliminates a source of personal revulsion and abuse through an extreme, harmful act.

Event: Has sex with Sawyer after seeing Jack close to Juliet
Explanation: Seeks emotional/sexual solace while using Sawyer in a love-triangle dynamic.

Event: Uses names of saints as aliases
Explanation: Stylized aliases still mainly serve evasion and deception.

Event: Becomes more of a hero over time through character development
Explanation: Her growth improves her life and the welfare of others.

Event: Tracks down Sawyer’s daughter Clementine after leaving the island
Explanation: Provides connection and support to Clementine, also honoring Sawyer.

Event: Acts as “Auntie Kate” to Clementine
Explanation: Offers ongoing affectionate support, enriching both their lives.

Event: Adopts Aaron after Claire disappears
Explanation: Takes responsibility for a vulnerable child, giving him care and family.

Event: Rebels against being told what to do
Explanation: Her contrariness preserves autonomy but often undermines group plans.

Event: Saves young Ben after Sayid shoots him
Explanation: Again chooses to preserve a child’s life despite known future risks.

Event: Uses sexualized “fanservice” moments to navigate interactions (e.g., with Sawyer, Charlie)
Explanation: Incidental or strategic use of attractiveness can gain leverage while distracting/impacting others.
